warning_signs.label: Severe Headache
warning_signs.detail: A severe headache that does not go away or worsens over time. Per CDC Hear Her, this may indicate a dangerous rise in blood pressure and requires immediate evaluation.

warning_signs.label: Changes in Vision
warning_signs.detail: Blurred vision, flashing lights, or floaters in your field of vision. Per CDC Hear Her, these visual changes can signal a serious postpartum complication such as preeclampsia.

warning_signs.label: Chest Pain or Trouble Breathing
warning_signs.detail: Chest pain, pressure, or difficulty breathing that is new or worsening. Per CDC Hear Her, these symptoms require emergency evaluation and may indicate pulmonary embolism or postpartum cardiomyopathy.

warning_signs.label: Seizures
warning_signs.detail: Any seizure activity in the postpartum period requires immediate emergency response. Per CDC Hear Her, postpartum seizures may indicate eclampsia. Activate emergency services and transfer to emergency care immediately.

warning_signs.label: Fever
warning_signs.detail: A fever of 100.4°F (38°C) or higher in the postpartum period. Per CDC Hear Her, postpartum fever may indicate endometritis, wound infection, or other serious infection requiring prompt evaluation.

warning_signs.label: Leg Swelling, Redness, or Pain
warning_signs.detail: Swelling, redness, warmth, or pain in one or both legs. Per CDC Hear Her, these signs may indicate deep vein thrombosis — a dangerous blood clot that can travel to the lungs.

warning_signs.label: Swelling in Face or Hands
warning_signs.detail: Sudden or severe swelling in the face or hands. Per CDC Hear Her, this may be a sign of postpartum preeclampsia, which can occur up to 6 weeks after delivery and requires immediate evaluation.

warning_signs.label: Extreme Fatigue or Shortness of Breath
warning_signs.detail: Extreme exhaustion or shortness of breath beyond normal postpartum tiredness. Per CDC Hear Her, these symptoms may indicate postpartum cardiomyopathy or severe anemia and require clinical evaluation.

warning_signs.label: Thoughts of Self-Harm or Harming Baby
warning_signs.detail: Any reported thoughts of self-harm or harm to the infant. Per CDC Hear Her, these require immediate clinical response. Screen urgently using a validated tool, provide safety planning, and connect to behavioral health resources and crisis support.

warning_signs.label: Severe Sadness or Hopelessness
warning_signs.detail: Feeling very sad, hopeless, or unable to bond with your baby for more than a few days. Per CDC Hear Her, these may be signs of postpartum depression and require evaluation, support, and treatment.

warning_signs.label: Heavy Postpartum Bleeding
warning_signs.detail: Soaking more than one pad per hour or passing large clots after the first few days. Per CDC Hear Her, heavy postpartum bleeding is a medical emergency — call 911.

warning_signs.label: Severe Abdominal, Back, or Pelvic Pain
warning_signs.detail: Severe or persistent pain in the abdomen, back, or pelvis that does not improve. Per CDC Hear Her, this may indicate infection, internal bleeding, or other serious complications requiring immediate care.
